g.text: Valuation & Conclusions
802 Bankview Dr, Drumheller, Alberta | File VAL251026
Unit Rent Discussion
Due to the lack of available rental units in the Drumheller market we have included other markets in close proximity
to the subject market. A total renovation budget of $301,486 is proposed, including $230,386 for interior suite
upgrades, $32,500 for common-area paint and lighting, and $38,600 for boiler replacement. The majority of spending
is focused on modernizing interiors, which is the primary factor influencing rent potential relative to the competitive
market.
1-Bedroom Rent Analysis
Current as-is rents sit at the lower end of the comparable range, where older buildings with basic finishes typically
achieve $1,140–$1,250. Renovated 1-bedroom rents of $1,225 align more closely with mid-market comparables that
feature upgraded interiors. While still below the highest-achieving comps, the renovated rents remain well
supported by the observed market data.
2-Bedroom Rent Analysis
The as-is rents fall near the bottom of the comparable 2-bedroom range ($1,225–$1,600), consistent with the
subject’s older finishes. Renovated rents at $1,450 position the units more competitively alongside mid-quality
comparables offering updated interiors, though still below top-end newer product. These renovated rents remain
supported by the midpoint of the comparable rent spectrum.
Conclusion
Across both unit types, the rent comps demonstrate that the subject’s as-is rents reflect its current position as an
older, average-quality building. The proposed renovations support movement toward the center of the comparable
market range, with achievable increases to $1,225 for 1-bedrooms and $1,450 for 2-bedrooms, consistent with rents
observed among upgraded or moderately renovated competing properties.
Contract Versus Market Rent
Based on the previous conclusions, the subject’s average contract rent is 86.4% of market rents as if renovated.
Total Rental Revenue - Multi-Family
The Total Rental Revenue for the Multi-Family component is summarized in the table below. The subject’s average
contract rent is 86.4% of market rents. Market rents are applied in our analysis.
